userStories.title: Single-Channel Capture
userStories.description: User can capture thoughts by posting messages to a single, private Slack channel
acceptanceCriteria: Bot monitors configured Slack channel
All user messages are captured
Bot messages are ignored
Thread replies are captured for corrections

userStories.title: Zero-Decision Capture
userStories.description: User can capture raw thoughts without any formatting, categorization, or metadata
acceptanceCriteria: Plain text messages are accepted
No special syntax required
No metadata needed from user
AI handles all classification

userStories.title: Automatic Categorization
userStories.description: System automatically categorizes captured thoughts into people, projects, ideas, or admin
acceptanceCriteria: AI classifies each message
Categories: people, projects, ideas, admin
Classification happens within 10 seconds
Confidence score assigned

userStories.title: Field Extraction
userStories.description: System extracts type-specific fields from each captured thought
acceptanceCriteria: People: name, context, follow-ups
Projects: name, status, next action, notes
Ideas: title, oneliner, notes
Admin: name, due date, status

userStories.title: Confidence Scoring
userStories.description: AI provides confidence score for each classification
acceptanceCriteria: Scores range from 0.0 to 1.0
Low confidence triggers review flow
Threshold configurable
Score displayed in confirmation

userStories.title: Audit Log System
userStories.description: Create comprehensive JSON audit log tracking all captured thoughts with UUID, timestamps, classification results, and status
acceptanceCriteria: All entries have unique UUID and ISO-8601 timestamp
Schema includes: original_text, destination, title, confidence, fields, status, model
Support filtering by date range, category, and status
Atomic append operations to prevent data loss
Support correction updates with full audit trail

userStories.title: Filing Confirmation
userStories.description: Send Slack thread reply after filing with checkmark emoji, destination, title, confidence score, and correction instructions
acceptanceCriteria: Reply posted in thread within 5 seconds of filing
Message includes: ✅ emoji, category, title, confidence score
Includes instructions: 'Reply 'fix: <correction>' if wrong'
Low-confidence items handled differently (see US-008)
Removes current captured_thoughts.jsonl logic

userStories.title: Low-Confidence Handling
userStories.description: DM user for clarification when classification confidence is below threshold instead of filing
acceptanceCriteria: Configurable confidence threshold (default: 0.7)
Low-confidence items NOT written to category files
Logged to audit with status='needs_review'
DM sent to user with original message, confidence score, and clarification request
DM includes examples of how to repost with hints

userStories.title: One-Step Corrections
userStories.description: Parse and apply 'fix:' commands from thread replies to correct categorization, title, or other fields
acceptanceCriteria: Supports: 'fix: this should be people, not projects'
Supports: 'fix: move to ideas'
Supports: 'fix: change title to "Better title"'
Removes entry from old category file
Adds entry to new category file
Updates audit log with correction details
Updates timeline links
Posts confirmation in thread

userStories.title: Error Recovery
userStories.description: Implement retry logic, exponential backoff, and user notifications for LLM API failures, markdown write failures, and Slack API failures
acceptanceCriteria: LLM API Failure: Preserve in audit log, notify user, retry with backoff (max 3)
Markdown Write Failure: Preserve classification in audit, notify with error, offer retry
Slack API Failure: Still write to markdown and log, continue processing
After 3 LLM failures, ask user to manually categorize via Slack DM
After markdown write failure, offer interactive recovery with yes/no prompt
All errors logged with full context
userStories.notes: P1 - Parent story - Child stories: US-017, US-018. Error recovery flows implemented.

userStories.title: Manual Categorization After LLM Failures
userStories.description: After 3 failed LLM classification attempts, prompt user to manually categorize via Slack DM with category prefixes (person:, project:, idea:, admin:)
acceptanceCriteria: After 3 LLM retry failures, send DM requesting manual categorization
User can reply with category prefix: 'person:', 'project:', 'idea:', 'admin:'
Bot processes manual categorization and files entry normally
Original message preserved in audit log
User receives confirmation when manual categorization complete
Audit log tracks manual categorization flow

userStories.title: Interactive Recovery for Markdown Write Failures
userStories.description: When markdown write fails, offer interactive recovery mode with 'Would you like me to retry? (yes/no)' prompt
acceptanceCriteria: After markdown write fails, send DM with error details
Offer recovery mode: 'Would you like me to retry? (yes/no)'
If user responds 'yes', retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
If user responds 'no' or timeout, preserve data in audit log with status='error'
Classification data never lost (always in audit log)
Processing continues for other messages
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-011-interactive-recovery.md - Part of US-010

userStories.title: Daily Summary Generation
userStories.description: Automatically generate end-of-day summary via LLM at midnight in user's timezone and add to daily timeline file
acceptanceCriteria: Daily file is created at timeline/yyyy-mm-dd.md when first note captured
Each subsequent note appended to daily file throughout the day
At end of day (midnight), LLM generates 2-3 sentence summary
Summary based on all notes created that day
Summary added to daily file frontmatter and as content section
Process automated via cron job running daily at midnight
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-012-daily-summary-generation.md - Part of US-016

userStories.title: Weekly Review Content to Timeline Files
userStories.description: Write weekly review content (highlights, open loops, suggested actions) to weekly timeline file
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly file created/updated at timeline/yyyy-W##.md when weekly review runs
Full weekly review content included (from Slack digest)
Review includes: what happened, biggest open loops, 3 suggested actions
Links to all 7 daily notes for that week
Aggregated statistics and patterns
File format matches Weekly Note Schema from PRD
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-013-weekly-review-content.md - Part of US-016

userStories.title: Monthly Review Content to Timeline Files
userStories.description: Write monthly review content with trends and patterns to monthly timeline file
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly file created/updated at timeline/yyyy-mm.md when monthly review runs
Monthly review includes trends and patterns
Links to all weekly notes for that month
Aggregated statistics (total notes, most active day, category breakdown)
Top accomplishments and goals for next month
File format matches Monthly Note Schema from PRD
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-014-monthly-review-content.md - Part of US-016

userStories.title: Quarterly Review Content to Timeline Files
userStories.description: Write quarterly strategic review with themes and questions to quarterly timeline file
acceptanceCriteria: Quarterly file created/updated at timeline/yyyy-Q#.md when quarterly review runs
Quarterly file includes strategic review with themes and questions
Links to all monthly notes for that quarter
Top 5 projects by impact (accomplishments, progress)
Goals for next quarter based on current quarter's learnings
File format matches Quarterly Note Schema from PRD
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-015-quarterly-review-content.md - Part of US-016

userStories.title: Yearly Review Content to Timeline Files
userStories.description: Write comprehensive annual review with productivity metrics to yearly timeline file
acceptanceCriteria: Yearly file created/updated at timeline/yyyy.md when yearly review runs
Yearly file includes comprehensive annual review
Links to all quarterly notes for that year
Top 10 completed projects by impact
Productivity metrics (capture consistency, system health)
Annual highlights and accomplishments
Goals for next year based on current year's learnings
File format matches Yearly Note Schema from PRD
userStories.notes: P1 - From SPEC-016-yearly-review-content.md - Part of US-016

userStories.title: Markdown Writer
userStories.description: Write entries to category markdown files (people.md, projects.md, ideas.md, admin.md) with YAML frontmatter and proper formatting
acceptanceCriteria: Generate YAML frontmatter with all schema fields
Generate markdown body with headers
Append to category file with '---' separator
Use atomic file operations (write to temp, then rename)
Support removeEntry() for corrections
Support updateEntry() for in-place corrections
Entry templates match PRD schema per category

userStories.title: File Organization
userStories.description: Create and maintain directory structure for second-brain including category files, audit log, timeline, and archive directories
acceptanceCriteria: Create second-brain/people.md (empty with header)
Create second-brain/projects.md (empty with header)
Create second-brain/ideas.md (empty with header)
Create second-brain/admin.md (empty with header)
Create second-brain/inbox-log.json (empty array)
Create second-brain/timeline/ directory
Create second-brain/archive/ directory
Create second-brain/README.md explaining structure
All operations use atomic file operations
All operations return Result<AppError, T>

userStories.title: Git Integration
userStories.description: Automatically commit changes to second-brain with formatted commit messages
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-commit after each entry is filed
Commit message format: 'File {destination}: {title}'
Include timestamp in commit message or body
Stage changed files before commit
Initialize git repo if not exists
Check if directory is git repo before operations
All operations return Result<AppError, T>

userStories.title: Daily Digest Generation
userStories.description: Generate daily digest (<150 words) with top 3 actions, one thing to avoid, and one small win
acceptanceCriteria: Query active projects, people with follow-ups, admin due today
Send to LLM with digest prompt
Return: Top 3 actions, one thing avoiding, one small win
Validate word count before returning (<150 words)
Generated daily at configured time
Sent via Slack DM to user

userStories.title: Weekly Review Generation
userStories.description: Generate weekly review (<250 words) with highlights, open loops, 3 suggested actions, and recurring theme
acceptanceCriteria: Query last 7 days of audit log, active projects, people
Send to LLM with weekly review prompt
Return: Highlights, open loops, 3 suggested actions, recurring theme
Validate word count before returning (<250 words)
Generated weekly at configured day/time
Sent via Slack DM to user

userStories.title: Timeline Management
userStories.description: Create and maintain daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly timeline files with proper linking
acceptanceCriteria: Create timeline/yyyy-mm-dd.md with YAML frontmatter
Include date, day_of_week, week_number, quarter
Create timeline/yyyy-W##.md with date range and links to daily notes
Create timeline/yyyy-mm.md, yyyy-Q#.md, yyyy.md files
Append notes to daily files
Link daily → weekly → monthly → quarterly → yearly
Generate end-of-day summary via LLM and add to daily file
Write weekly review content to weekly timeline file
Write monthly review content with trends and patterns
Write quarterly strategic review with themes and questions
Write yearly comprehensive review with productivity metrics
Use date-fns for date calculations
All operations return Result<AppError, T>
userStories.notes: P1 - Parent story - Child stories: US-019, US-020, US-021, US-022, US-023. 100% complete - All AI summaries and reviews implemented (US-019 through US-023)

userStories.title: Cross-Linking
userStories.description: Create bidirectional links between category files and timeline files
acceptanceCriteria: Generate heading IDs by slugifying titles for anchors
Link in daily file: [title](../category.md#heading)
Link in category file: Captured: [date](timeline/yyyy-mm-dd.md)
Link timeline hierarchy: day → week → month → quarter → year
All links use markdown format with relative paths
All operations return Result<AppError, T>

userStories.title: Timeline Navigation
userStories.description: Provide easy navigation between timeline levels and related entries
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate between day, week, month, quarter, year views
Jump to specific dates from category entries
View all entries for a time period
Search within timeline
Filter timeline by category

userStories.title: Digest Scheduling
userStories.description: Schedule daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly digest generation with cron
acceptanceCriteria: Schedule daily digest at configurable time
Schedule weekly review at configurable day and time
Schedule monthly review (last day of month)
Schedule quarterly review (last day of quarter)
Schedule yearly review (December 31)
Use node-cron for scheduling
Support stopping all scheduled jobs
All operations return Result<AppError, T>

userStories.title: First-Time Setup Wizard
userStories.description: Interactive setup wizard for initial configuration including Slack tokens, LLM provider, channel, and directory paths
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt for Slack bot token and app token
Prompt for LLM provider choice (claude, openai, deepseek, local)
Prompt for LLM API key (if applicable)
Prompt for Slack channel name
Prompt for markdown directory path
Create config file with validated settings
Initialize directory structure
Create audit log
Send test message to verify Slack connection
Complete setup in <30 minutes
Use interactive prompts (readline or similar)

userStories.title: Multi-Provider Voice Capture
userStories.description: Implement multi-provider voice capture functionality enabling users to dictate voice notes in Slack that are transcribed and processed through the existing classification pipeline. Supports OpenAI Whisper (default), GLM, Google Cloud Speech-to-Text, DeepSeek, and local Whisper.cpp transcription services.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio messages detected in Slack inbox channel
Files downloaded and validated (format, size limits)
Transcription via OpenAI Whisper API (default provider)
Provider switching via configuration (openai, glm, google, deepseek, local)
Transcribed text classified by existing LLM
Entries stored with audio metadata in audit log
Confirmation messages include transcript preview
Error handling with graceful degradation (inbox_log fallback)
End-to-end processing < 30s for 1-minute audio
Zero audio file persistence (privacy)
Multi-provider transcription client factory implemented
File downloader service for Slack audio files
Configuration validation for all providers
Unit tests for transcription client
Integration tests for full pipeline
userStories.notes: From SPEC-017-voice-capture.md - Multi-provider support with OpenAI Whisper as default. Implements TDD with tests first, then implementation. ~26 hours over 6 phases.

userStories.title: Natural Language Search
userStories.description: Search across all category files using natural language queries
acceptanceCriteria: Parse natural language query
Search across all category files
Rank results by relevance
Return matching entries with context
Support queries like: 'What did I say about Sarah?'
Support queries like: 'Active projects related to website'
Support queries like: 'Ideas from last week'

userStories.title: Fix Search Command Conflict
userStories.description: Change search command prefix from 'search:' to '!search' to avoid conflicts with Slack's slash command system which uses '/' prefix
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/bot.ts to use '!search' prefix instead of 'search:'
Update README.md examples to show '!search' command
Update prd.md documentation to use '!search' syntax
Update specs/SPEC-003-complete-remaining-stories.md to use '!search'
Update src/AGENTS.md to reflect new command syntax
Ensure all references to 'search:' are replaced with '!search'
Run typecheck to verify no TypeScript errors
Run full test suite to ensure no regressions
